en: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：

en: "Speak to Aaron and his sons so that they abstain from the holy things of the people of Israel, which they dedicate to me, so that they do not profane my holy name: I am the LORD.
zh: “你要告訴亞倫和他的兒子，叫他們謹慎處理以色列人分別為聖歸給我的聖物，免得他們褻瀆我的聖名；我是耶和華。

en: Say to them, 'If any one of all your offspring throughout your generations approaches the holy things that the people of Israel dedicate to the LORD, while he has an uncleanness, that person shall be cut off from my presence: I am the LORD.
zh: 你要對他們說：你們世世代代的後裔中，若是不潔淨，卻接近以色列人分別為聖歸耶和華的聖物，那人必從我面前被剪除；我是耶和華。

en: None of the offspring of Aaron who has a leprous disease or a discharge may eat of the holy things until he is clean. Whoever touches anything that is unclean through contact with the dead or a man who has had an emission of semen,
zh: 亞倫的後裔中，患了痲風病，或是血漏症的，都不可吃聖物，直到他潔淨了；觸摸了任何屍體的不潔或遺精的人，

en: and whoever touches a swarming thing by which he may be made unclean or a person from whom he may take uncleanness, whatever his uncleanness may be-
zh: 或是觸摸了任何使他玷污的爬蟲，或是摸了使他成為不潔淨的人 ，

en: the person who touches such a thing shall be unclean until the evening and shall not eat of the holy things unless he has bathed his body in water.
zh: 摸了這些的人，必不潔淨到晚上；如果他不用水洗身，就不可吃聖物。

en: When the sun goes down he shall be clean, and afterward he may eat of the holy things, because they are his food.
zh: 日落之後，他就潔淨了，可以吃聖物，因為那是他的食物。

en: He shall not eat what dies of itself or is torn by beasts, and so make himself unclean by it: I am the LORD.'
zh: 自死的或是野獸撕裂的動物，他都不可吃，免得玷污自己；我是耶和華。

en: They shall therefore keep my charge, lest they bear sin for it and die thereby when they profane it: I am the LORD who sanctifies them.
zh: 他們要謹守我的命令，免得他們因褻瀆，擔罪而死；我是使他們分別為聖的耶和華。

en: "A lay person shall not eat of a holy thing; no foreign guest of the priest or hired servant shall eat of a holy thing,
zh: “所有外族人都不可吃聖物；寄居在祭司家裡的人或是受雇的雇工，都不可吃聖物。

en: but if a priest buys a slave as his property for money, the slave may eat of it, and anyone born in his house may eat of his food.
zh: 但是祭司用自己銀子買來的僕人，就可以吃聖物；生在祭司家裡的人，他們可以吃他的食物。

en: If a priest's daughter marries a layman, she shall not eat of the contribution of the holy things.
zh: 祭司的女兒若是嫁給外族人，就不可吃舉祭的聖物。

en: But if a priest's daughter is widowed or divorced and has no child and returns to her father's house, as in her youth, she may eat of her father's food; yet no lay person shall eat of it.
zh: 但祭司的女兒若是寡婦，或是被休的，而且沒有孩子，又回到自己的父家，像她年輕的時候一樣，她就可以吃她父親的食物；只是任何外族人都不可吃。

en: And if anyone eats of a holy thing unintentionally, he shall add the fifth of its value to it and give the holy thing to the priest.
zh: 人若是誤吃了聖物，就要加上五分之一，與聖物一起交給祭司。

en: They shall not profane the holy things of the people of Israel, which they contribute to the LORD,
zh: 祭司不可褻瀆以色列人獻上給耶和華的聖物，

en: and so cause them to bear iniquity and guilt, by eating their holy things: for I am the LORD who sanctifies them."
zh: 免得他們因吃了聖物而擔當罪孽過犯；因為我是使他們分別為聖的耶和華。”

en: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：

en: "Speak to Aaron and his sons and all the people of Israel and say to them, When any one of the house of Israel or of the sojourners in Israel presents a burnt offering as his offering, for any of their vows or freewill offerings that they offer to the LORD,
zh: “你要告訴亞倫和亞倫的兒子，以及所有以色列人，對他們說：任何以色列家的人，或是在以色列中寄居的外人，奉獻供物的時候，無論是為還願，或是甘心獻給耶和華的燔祭，

en: if it is to be accepted for you it shall be a male without blemish, of the bulls or the sheep or the goats.
zh: 就要把沒有殘疾的公牛，或公綿羊，或公山羊獻上，才蒙悅納。

en: You shall not offer anything that has a blemish, for it will not be acceptable for you.
zh: 有殘疾的，你們都不可獻上，因為這是不蒙悅納的。

en: And when anyone offers a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering from the herd or from the flock, to be accepted it must be perfect; there shall be no blemish in it.
zh: 人若是為了還願，或是甘心把牛羊獻給耶和華作平安祭，總要獻上一隻完全、沒有任何瑕疵的祭物，才蒙悅納。

en: Animals blind or disabled or mutilated or having a discharge or an itch or scabs you shall not offer to the LORD or give them to the LORD as a food offering on the altar.
zh: 瞎眼、折傷、殘廢、傷口潰瘍、長癬或生疹的牲畜，你們都不可獻給耶和華，不可把這些當作火祭放在祭壇上獻給耶和華，

en: You may present a bull or a lamb that has a part too long or too short for a freewill offering, but for a vow offering it cannot be accepted.
zh: 無論是公牛或是綿羊羔，如果肢體畸形或有缺陷的，你可以作甘心祭獻上；如果作還願祭獻上，就不蒙悅納。

en: Any animal that has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn or cut you shall not offer to the LORD; you shall not do it within your land,
zh: 內臟損傷、壓碎、破裂，或割掉的，你們都不可獻與耶和華；在你們將來居住的地方，你們不可行這樣的事；

en: neither shall you offer as the bread of your God any such animals gotten from a foreigner. Since there is a blemish in them, because of their mutilation, they will not be accepted for you."
zh: 也不可從外人手裡接受這一類的牲畜，作你們 神的食物；因為這些都有損壞，有殘疾，必不蒙悅納。”

en: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：

en: "When an ox or sheep or goat is born, it shall remain seven days with its mother, and from the eighth day on it shall be acceptable as a food offering to the LORD.
zh: “公牛或是綿羊或是山羊生下來以後，七天要與牠的母親在一起，從第八天開始，牠就可以蒙悅納，作獻給耶和華火祭的供物。

en: But you shall not kill an ox or a sheep and her young in one day.
zh: 無論是牛或羊，你們都不可在同一日之內宰殺母和子。

en: And when you sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the LORD, you shall sacrifice it so that you may be accepted.
zh: 如果你們獻感恩祭給耶和華，要獻得可蒙悅納。

en: It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall leave none of it until morning: I am the LORD.
zh: 祭肉要當天吃完，一點都不可留到早晨；我是耶和華。

en: "So you shall keep my commandments and do them: I am the LORD.
zh: 你們要謹守遵行我的誡命；我是耶和華。

en: And you shall not profane my holy name, that I may be sanctified among the people of Israel. I am the LORD who sanctifies you,
zh: 你們不可褻瀆我的聖名，我在以色列人中間要被尊為聖；我是使你們分別為聖的耶和華，

en: who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I am the LORD."
zh: 曾把你們從埃及地領出來，要作你們的 神；我是耶和華。”
